title: 10 best herbal decoctions for overactive bladder
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for overactive bladder are concentrated liquid extracts made from a combination of herbs, which have been used to alleviate symptoms associated with urinary incontinence. These decoctions help by soothing the bladder and urethral muscles, reducing frequency and urgency of urination. Examples of herbs used include horsetail, marshmallow root, and chamomile, which can be brewed into a tea or taken as a supplement. By using herbal decoctions, individuals with overactive bladder can experience improved bladder control, reduced incontinence episodes, and enhanced overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bearberry decoctions helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to strengthen the muscles in the urinary tract. The anthraquinone compounds present in bearberry, specifically aloe-emodin and chrysophanol, have been shown to increase the tone of the bladder musculature, reducing the frequency and urgency of urination. Additionally, bearberry's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the bladder lining and reduce irritation, alleviating discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective way to manage overactive bladder symptoms without relying on pharmaceutical interventions.
remedy_properties: Uropodophyllotoxin: Bearberry's uropodophyllotoxin content has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relax the smooth muscles in the urinary tract and reduce bladder spasms, alleviating symptoms of overactive bladder.

Arbutin: The high concentration of arbutin in bearberry decoctions inhibits the growth of bacteria that can contribute to urinary tract infections (UTIs), which are common comorbidities with overactive bladder, and may also help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with UTIs.

Tannins: Bearberry's tannins have astringent properties that can help reduce the frequency and urgency of urination by contracting the bladder muscles and reducing the sensitivity of nerve endings in the urinary tract.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bearberry plant contain anthraquinone glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing the urinary tract and reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Barks: The barks of the bearberry plant contain tannins, which help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract, providing relief from frequent urination and urgency.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arctostaphylos uva ursi leaves measuring one cup for every quart of water.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the rinsed leaves with four cups of purified water in a large pot or teapot.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it provides a soothing and protective coating to the bladder lining, reducing irritation and inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow root also help to calm the muscles in the urinary tract, allowing for more efficient emptying of the bladder and reduced frequency of trips to the bathroom. Additionally, marshmallow decoctions can help to reduce urgency and burning sensations associated with overactive bladder, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow's mucilage property helps to soothe and protect the urinary tract lining, reducing inflammation and irritation that can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The decoction contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the bladder and urethra, alleviating discomfort and frequency of urination.

Emollients: Marshmallow's emollient properties help to lubricate and soften the mucous membranes lining the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps soothe and calm irritated bladder tissues.
Seeds: Containing saponins that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh althaea officinalis leaves or purchase dried ones from a reputable source for use.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried leaf material or three teaspoons of fresh leaf material per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herb material with two cups of boiling water in a large glass container to create the decoction mixture.
Allow the decoction mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes, then strain it using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container.
Discard the solids and store the strained liquid in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod decoctions helps with overactive bladder because its active compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, have a soothing effect on the urinary tract. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urethra, alleviating symptoms of urgency and frequency. Additionally, goldenrod's natural antibacterial properties may also help to eliminate underlying infections that can contribute to overactive bladder. As a result, regular consumption of goldenrod decoctions may provide relief from frequent urination and other uncomfortable symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Uterotropic properties: Goldenrod's uterotropic properties help to relax and tone the muscles of the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of urgency and frequency associated with overactive bladder.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Goldenrod's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as burning or discomfort during urination.
Antispasmodic properties: Goldenrod's antispasmodic properties may help relax smooth muscle spasms in the urinary tract, reducing contractions that can lead to increased frequency and urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Goldenrod roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help soothe and calm the urinary tract muscles.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of goldenrod contain bioactive compounds that have been shown to relax smooth muscle contractions, reducing frequency and urgency of urination.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried solidago virgaurea herb per cup of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat for 10 minutes steeping time.
Strain the liquid and discard solids or compost them properly.
Store the decoction in an airtight container refrigerated at 40 degrees fahrenheit.
Consume the decoction three times a day with meals for two weeks treatment duration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it is rich in silicon, which has been shown to strengthen the bladder lining and reduce inflammation. The decoction's antioxidant properties also help to soothe the urinary tract and reduce irritation. Additionally, the flavonoids present in field horsetail have a natural ability to relax the muscles surrounding the bladder, allowing for improved urine flow and reduced urge to urinate. This combination of properties makes field horsetail decoctions an effective natural remedy for managing overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Potassium content: Horsetail's high potassium levels help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that contribute to overactive bladder symptoms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Horsetail's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms such as urgency and frequency.

Antioxidant activity: Horsetail's antioxidant properties help to protect the tissues of the urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall bladder health and reducing the risk of chronic inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can help alleviate symptoms of an overactive bladder.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat urinary issues, including frequency and urgency, likely due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried equisetum arvense herb in the morning after dew has fallen, gathering at least one ounce of material for each dose.
Rinse the harvested herb gently with cold running water, then chop it into small pieces to release its medicinal properties.
Combine 8 ounces of chopped equisetum arvense with 4 cups of distilled water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer for 20-30 minutes, or until the decoction has reduced by half and its color has deepened.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it has been traditionally used to support urinary tract health and relieve symptoms such as frequent urination, urgency, and nocturia. The natural compounds in saw palmetto may help soothe an overactive bladder by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation in the urinary tract, allowing for more control over bladder function. By calming the muscles in the urethra and bladder, saw palmetto decoctions can reduce symptoms of urge incontinence and improve overall bladder health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Saw palmetto's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as frequency, urgency, and discomfort.

Muscle relaxant effects: The flavonoids and phytosterols present in saw palmetto decoctions have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle cells in the urinary tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder by reducing muscle spasms and increasing bladder capacity.

Pro-inflammatory mediator inhibition: Saw palmetto has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators such as cytokines and prostaglandins, which are involved in the pathophysiology of overactive bladder. By inhibiting these mediators, saw palmetto may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the urinary tract and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Saw palmetto roots are used to make medicinal decoctions because they contain fatty acids and sterols that help relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.

Fruits: The berries or fruits of the saw palmetto plant are used to create decoctions for overactive bladder as they have been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help reduce muscle spasms in the urinary tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried serenoa repens leaves and one quart of cold water.
Combine the dried leaves with the cold water in a large saucepan.
Heat the mixture on low heat for 20-25 minutes or until the liquid reaches 200°f.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool before refrigerating it at a temperature of 39°f.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger decoctions helps with overactive bladder because its natural compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have a soothing effect on the urinary tract. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall, allowing for improved urine storage capacity and reducing the frequency of urination. Additionally, ginger's antioxidant properties help to protect against oxidative stress, which can contribute to bladder dysfunction. As a result, regular consumption of herbal ginger decoctions may provide relief from overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Ginger decoction helps relax the bladder muscles, reducing spasms and frequency of urination due to its ability to inhibit calcium channels, which can cause muscle contractions.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms such as burning and urgency during urination.
Muscle-relaxing property: Ginger's flavonoids help relax the smooth muscle in the bladder wall and urethra, increasing bladder capacity and reducing the sensation of urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have a calming effect on the urinary tract and help reduce symptoms of overactive bladder.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of the ginger plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the bladder muscles.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger rhizomes of 10-12 months old and wash them thoroughly under cold running water.
Peel the ginger rhizomes using a vegetable peeler removing any rough or damaged skin.
Slice the peeled ginger into thin pieces about 1/8 inch thick to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one teaspoon of sliced ginger with two cups of boiling water and allow it to steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container discarding solids and storing in refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it has a natural diuretic effect, increasing urine production and reducing frequency of urination. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also help soothe the urinary tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with overactive bladder symptoms. Additionally, dandelion's ability to balance electrolytes and fluids can help regulate the bladder's muscle contractions, providing relief from frequent trips to the bathroom. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to support urinary health and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion's diuretic effect helps to increase urine production, which can help to reduce symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and urgency.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms, and also alleviate pain and discomfort associated with urinary frequency.

Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant activity helps to protect the bladder and urinary tract from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms, and also promote overall urinary health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the dandelion plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm the urinary tract, reducing symptoms of overactive bladder.
Fruits: Dandelion fruits have been traditionally used to treat urinary issues, including incontinence, as they are believed to possess antispasmodic properties that relax the muscles in the urethra.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and roots, then rinse thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the leaves and roots into small pieces and combine in a large pot.
Add one quart of purified water for every ounce of dried taraxacum officinale material used.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 30-40 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with overactive bladder because of its ability to soothe the urinary tract and reduce inflammation. The flavonoids present in licorice root have a direct effect on the smooth muscle surrounding the bladder, relaxing it and reducing contractions that can lead to frequent urination. Additionally, the antioxidants in licorice decoctions help to combat oxidative stress and damage caused by chronic inflammation, further reducing symptoms of overactive bladder. Muscle relaxant and anti-anxiety properties: Herbal licorice decoctions have been traditionally used to calm nervous tension and reduce muscle spasms, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that can trigger or worsen overactive bladder symptoms.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the licorice plant are used to make decoctions that can help to calm the urinary tract and reduce spasms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried licorice root extract of glycyrrhiza glabra measure 3 grams.
Grind the dried licorice root into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground licorice root with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a saucepan.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes or until it reaches the desired strength and flavor.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with overactive bladder because of its ability to relax the urinary tract muscles, reducing spasms and frequency. The antispasmodic properties in yarrow help calm the nerves that control urine flow, allowing for a smoother and more controlled release. Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the bladder and urethra, which can contribute to overactive bladder symptoms like pain and urgency.

Uric acid reducing properties: Yarrow has been shown to have uric acid reducing properties, which can help to alleviate urinary tract infections (UTIs) and other conditions that can exacerbate overactive bladder symptoms, such as kidney stones and gout.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers for a recommended dosage of 2-3 teaspoons.
Rinse the plant material with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris before use.
Combine the herb with boiling water in a ratio of 1:4 parts herb to water, steep for 10-15 minutes to extract bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discard solids and reserve liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before drinking immediately, store in refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Juniper decoctions helps with overactive bladder because its active compounds, specifically terpenes and flavonoids, possess antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can lead to frequent urination and incontinence. Diuretic properties: The decoction can increase urine production and help flush out toxins from the body, which may reduce the frequency of urination and alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried juniperus communis berries in sufficient quantities for a 4-6 week supply of tea.
Measure out one teaspoon of the dried herb per cup of boiling water and add it to a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container.
Discard the solids and use the decoction as needed, up to three times daily, or store in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.
Consume the herbal tea within 6 weeks of preparation, making sure to label and date each batch for tracking purposes.

Buds: Nettle buds contain flavonoids and other polyphenols that help to calm the bladder lining, reducing irritability and frequency of urination.
remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried urtica dioica leaves and stems with two parts water.
Combine the ingredients in a pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes then strain the liquid.
Measure out 250ml of the decoction and discard any remaining solids.
Store the decoction in an amber glass bottle and refrigerate for up to 7 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose decoctions helps with overactive bladder because it contains flavonoids, particularly quercetin, which has a calming effect on the muscles in the urinary tract. Barks: The barks of the Guelder rose tree contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been traditionally used to treat urinary issues, including overactive bladder symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried viburnum opulus berries in late summer or early fall weighing approximately 100 grams.
Clean the berries gently removing any stems or debris and chop them into smaller pieces.
Combine the chopped berries with 500 milliliters of water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Boil the mixture for 20-30 minutes then reduce heat and simmer for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and storing the liquid in the refrigerator.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves for every 250ml of water.
Rinse the herbs thoroughly with cold water and drain well before use.
Combine the herbs with 250ml of boiling water in a saucepan and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding solids.
Cool the decoction to room temperature within 30-45 minutes before storing it in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with overactive bladder because of its natural ability to calm the bladder muscles and reduce muscle spasms. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves of mentha x piperita and clean them thoroughly with cool running water.
Measure out 20-30 grams of the cleaned peppermint leaves and combine with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat to low and simmer for 15 minutes allowing the herbs to steep.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a large glass container discarding solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to sit at room temperature for at least 2 hours or refrigerate overnight before consuming as needed.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 ounce of dried melissa officinalis leaves and stems or 2 ounces of fresh material.
Boil 500 milliliters of water in a pot for 5-7 minutes to create a decoction base.
Add the gathered melissa officinalis material to the boiling water and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers per cup of boiling water.
Combine the ingredients in a heat-resistant teapot or ceramic infuser and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Drink one cup of the cooled decoction three times a day as needed to alleviate overactive bladder symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves measure out two teaspoons of leaves per eight ounces of water.
Combine the measured leaves with cold water in a pot and bring to a boil reducing heat to a simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup discard solids and let cool slightly.
Drink one cup of the cooled decoction three times daily ideally an hour before meals and at bedtime.
Store any remaining decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours reheat as needed.

The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the bladder lining, decreasing irritability and urgency. Additionally, the phytochemicals in maidenhair tree may help to increase urine storage capacity by enhancing the contraction force of the bladder muscle, providing long-term relief from symptoms associated with overactive bladder.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant property helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the urinary tract, which can contribute to symptoms of overactive bladder.

Muscle relaxant: The flavonoids and terpenes present in Ginkgo biloba decoction have a relaxing effect on smooth muscle tissues, including those found in the urinary tract, which can help reduce spasms and improve urinary flow.

Urinary tract anti-inflammatory: Ginkgo biloba's decoction has been shown to inhibit inflammatory responses in the urinary tract, which may help alleviate symptoms of overactive bladder such as frequent urination and urgency.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in tannins and flavonoids, which help to strengthen the muscles in the urinary tract and reduce inflammation.
Barks: Contain compounds that help to relax the smooth muscle in the bladder and urinary tract, reducing frequency of urination.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried ginkgo biloba leaves in quantities of one teaspoon per cup of water.
Rinse the dried ginkgo biloba leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove any impurities.
Combine the rinsed ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 cup of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the ginkgo biloba mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain and discard the solids, then store the decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat overactive bladder with herbal decoctions is to combine ingredients that calm the nervous system, strengthen the urinary sphincter, and reduce inflammation. A traditional Chinese medicine formula typically includes herbs such as Cassia seed, Cinnamon bark, and Licorice root, which can be brewed into a decoction to help alleviate symptoms of urgency and frequency.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to overactive bladder that are treated with herbal decoctions are urinary incontinence, bedwetting, and nocturia. These conditions can be caused by various factors such as weakened pelvic muscles, hormone imbalances, or nerve damage. Herbal decoctions like Cassia tea, Cnidium tea, and Epimedium tea have been traditionally used to treat these conditions by strengthening the bladder and urethral sphincters, reducing urine frequency and urgency, and improving overall urinary function.
